title: Careers and skills in space
body: "  How to get a space job  UK Space Agency’s “Careers in space” leaflet   Read the UK Space Agency’s “Careers in space: the sky is not the limit” leaflet, for an overview of different careers you can pursue that are involved with space, and the skills required for a career in space.   UKSpace101  UKSpace101 from Space Careers UK gives an overview of the space sector and space careers, as well as guidance on finding roles.   Find out more about UKSpace101 for those new to the space sector.   Visit the European Space Agency website   Credit: ESA   The European Space Agency is our biggest partner in space. Visit their website to discover guidance for space careers.   Visit the National Space Academy website  The National Space Academy offers careers advice and information, and also runs careers conferences.   Find details on the National Space Academy website.   View space career profiles   You can access videos on a wide range of space careers on the website of the European Space Education Resources Office (ESERO-UK).    You can find further video and print career profiles here.   How to become an astronaut    The main route for UK citizens to become an astronaut is through the competitive European Space Agency (ESA) astronaut selection programme.   Find more about the selection programme.   Find a space job, work experience, apprenticeships or internships  Space Careers UK   Space Careers UK offers a job board which lists job, volunteer and internship opportunities at many companies across the space sector.  It also has a work experience page which gives advice on how to approach a work experience placement and lists opportunities that you can apply for.   Check the UK Space Sector Landscape Map for companies in your area     Identify and locate UK space-related companies and organisations with the UK Space Sector Landscape Map.   You can use it to contact local companies about work experience or to find companies that may be hiring or offering apprenticeships.  Find opportunities at the European Space Agency   ESA advertise jobs on their website. See the latest openings.     Find space apprenticeships   You can find general information about becoming an apprentice and help to find an apprenticeship at the government’s dedicated apprenticeship site.  The government also offers a search tool to find live apprenticeships.   STEM Learning Research Placements and Experiences   Find research placements and experiences from the Nuffield Foundation, STEM.org for students in Year 12 (or equivalent).   The Science and Technology Facilities Council work experience placements   Find out more about STFC work experience placements, offered to students in years 10-13 or equivalent each year.   Watch videos on space careers  UK Space Agency video guidance on space careers     Watch this video, which contains interviews with people working in the sector, for general advice if you’re considering a career in space.   Principia careers video   Watch this short film  from Astro Academy of the work that UK scientists and engineers are doing in the space sector, both in support of astronauts and in applications for life on Earth.  "
